description: Length-matched, certainty-balanced oracle facts for LLM hallucination with tool grounding.

facts.context:
Tool check: check_llm_claim('RAG eliminates hallucination') → FALSE.
RAG reduces but does not eliminate hallucination because:
facts.truth: models ignore retrieved context or hallucinate beyond it
distractors: retrieval errors propagate and amplify model confabulation
context window limits prevent full document comprehension
embedding similarity fails to capture semantic relevance

facts.context:
Tool check: check_llm_claim('scaling eliminates hallucination') → FALSE.
Larger LLMs still hallucinate because:
facts.truth: hallucination is fundamental to generative models, not a capacity gap
distractors: parameter count directly determines factual accuracy in all cases
training data quality matters more than architecture or objective
computational bottlenecks prevent larger models from self-correcting

facts.context:
Tool check: check_llm_claim('LLM confidence is calibrated') → FALSE.
LLM confidence is problematic because:
facts.truth: models express high confidence even when producing wrong outputs
distractors: temperature scaling during sampling distorts the probability signal
beam search decoding artificially inflates output token certainty
longer generation contexts accumulate per-token calibration errors

facts.context:
Tool check: check_llm_claim('models self-detect hallucination') → FALSE.
Automated hallucination detection is difficult because:
facts.truth: it requires external verification that is often unavailable
distractors: internal activation patterns contain unreliable confidence signals
token-level entropy estimation requires prohibitive compute overhead
attention weight analysis cannot distinguish facts from fabrication

facts.context:
Tool check: check_llm_claim('self-consistency guarantees correctness') → FALSE.
Self-consistency decoding (sampling + voting) helps with:
facts.truth: reducing variance in reasoning but not fixing systematic errors
distractors: improving token-level fluency without changing factual content
increasing diversity of generated text at the cost of coherence
amplifying rare correct samples when majority votes are biased

facts.context:
Tool check: check_llm_claim('RLHF solves saying I dont know') → FALSE.
Teaching LLMs to say 'I don't know' is difficult because:
facts.truth: training incentivizes plausible answers over admitting uncertainty
distractors: reward models penalize refusal responses during RLHF fine-tuning
human preference data rarely includes examples of ideal abstention
policy gradient optimization converges to confident response patterns
